title: Elixir Meta Programming
summary: Code as data, from quote/unquote to macros, hygiene, the use pattern, and compile-time code generation — then why a self-describing language fits the LLM-agentic era (tool catalogs, structured outputs, Igniter AST-patching), and when not to reach for a macro.

concepts.title: Code That Writes Code, for Agents

images.prompt: A serene, modern editorial illustration in flat vector style: an alchemist's desk at warm candlelight where a single handwritten line of code rises as vapour into a glass alembic, distills into a glowing branching tree of small labeled nodes (an abstract syntax tree), and condenses back down into fresh code on the page below. Warm ivory-parchment background, deep wine-magenta and transmutation-teal accents with gold-leaf highlights, generous whitespace, soft depth, no real text baked in — just suggestive shapes of code and a data-tree.
images.alt: An alchemist's desk where a line of code distills into a glowing tree of data and condenses back into code
images.caption: Metaprogramming: distil code into data, transform it, condense it back into code.

images.prompt: A clean, modern editorial tech illustration in flat vector with soft glow: on the left, a tidy wine-magenta declaration sheet of nested code blocks; from it, several small labeled cards (each a typed tool with a name and tiny argument rows) rise along glowing gold rails toward a luminous teal AI orb on the right that reaches out to pick one card. Warm ivory-parchment background, deep wine-magenta and transmutation-teal palette with gold-leaf highlights, generous whitespace, no real text — just suggestive shapes. The feeling of a human-written declaration being read and used by a machine mind.
images.alt: A human-written declaration on the left becoming a deck of typed tool cards an AI core reads
images.caption: Human declares the capability; introspection hands the model a typed catalog.
